title: Monetary Policy Report—January 2025
summary: The Bank of Canada reduced its overnight policy rate by 25 basis points to 3% and announced the end of quantitative tightening. Canadian inflation has been around 2% since August 2024 and is expected to remain near target over the projection horizon. The Bank projects Canadian economic growth to average 1.8% in 2025 and 2026, while identifying US tariff threats as a major source of uncertainty already affecting business confidence and the Canadian dollar.
key_points: Bank of Canada cut overnight policy rate by 25 bps to 3% (Bank Rate 3.25%, deposit rate 2.95%)
End of quantitative tightening announced alongside the rate cut
Canadian inflation at approximately 2% since August 2024; near-target inflation expected over projection horizon
Canadian GDP growth forecast: 1.8% in 2025 and 2026
Global economy expected to grow at around 3%, with US growth strong but moderating in 2026
US tariff threats identified as major uncertainty weighing on consumer/business confidence, investment, and contributing to Canadian dollar depreciation
Labour market remains soft with some signs of slowing wage growth; economy in modest excess supply
why_it_matters: This Bank of Canada report signals a continuation of easing monetary policy with inflation near target, while flagging trade policy uncertainty as the primary risk to the outlook. Market participants tracking Canadian dollar, bonds, and equities should note the rate cut and QT cessation for carry and duration implications, while monitoring tariff developments as a key swing factor for growth and inflation projections.
macro_context: The Bank of Canada is easing policy with inflation near target and excess supply in the economy. Growth is forecast to strengthen supported by past rate cuts. The main risk is US tariff escalation which could disrupt trade, depress the Canadian dollar, and push inflation above target through retaliation. Both upside and downside risks to the inflation outlook are considered roughly balanced absent new tariffs.

human_review.notes: Historical record from January 2025 (14+ months old). Slightly lowered confidence for market impact assessment due to age. Source contains policy assumptions and illustrative tariff scenarios but does not incorporate actual tariffs into baseline projections.

notes: Source is a quarterly Monetary Policy Report from the Bank of Canada. Published approximately 14 months before today. The report includes the rate decision, economic projections, and discussion of risks including an illustrative tariff scenario. Data available in CSV, JSON, and XML formats per the source.
